diagrams.title: Tangent Plane Linearization
diagrams.description: Shows how linearization approximates a surface near a point using the tangent plane
diagrams.purpose: Visualize linearization as geometric concept - tangent plane touches surface at one point and approximates locally

diagrams.title: Jacobian vs Hessian Matrix Structure
diagrams.description: Comparison of first-order (Jacobian) and second-order (Hessian) derivative matrices
elements: Show symmetry in Hessian for continuous functions
diagrams.purpose: Understand matrix structures used in Taylor expansions and their roles in optimization

diagrams.title: Critical Point Classification using Hessian
diagrams.description: Three scenarios: positive definite (minimum), negative definite (maximum), indefinite (saddle)
elements: U-shaped valley → positive definite Hessian → local minimum
Inverted U (hill top) → negative definite Hessian → local maximum
Saddle surface → indefinite Hessian → saddle point
diagrams.purpose: Connect mathematical conditions (Hessian properties) to geometric interpretation of critical points
